svg.text: CSL — CHINESE SIGN LANGUAGE
CHEROLOGY — EAST ASIAN SIGN PARAMETERS
CSL HANDSHAPES AND ICONICITY
CSL: many signs iconic traces of Chinese characters (unique to East Asian SLs)
PERSON: index fingers trace human outline (universal iconic sign)
Numbers 1-10: one-hand Chinese number system (fist=10, etc.)
Number signs: widely shared across East Asia (same counting tradition)
CSL MOVEMENT AND SPACE
CSL: uses signing space similar to other sign languages
Spatial loci: same grammatical function as ASL/LSF/DGS
CSL: some signs more body-anchored than neutral-space-heavy ASL
CSL NMM
NMM: same grammatical function (eyebrows, gaze, head movement)
Chinese mouthing: Mandarin words mouthed with signs (common in CSL)
Tonal mouthing: some signers mouth with Mandarin tones visible on face
KSL CHEROLOGY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
KSL HANDSHAPES
KSL handshapes: overlap with JSL (shared colonial-era origin)
Some CSL-influenced signs in KSL (historical Chinese cultural influence)
KSL modern development: diverged from JSL since 1945 independence
KSL SPATIAL GRAMMAR
KSL: same spatial grammar as other sign languages (universal)
Loci, verb agreement, classifiers: all present in KSL
KSL NMM: Korean mouthing common; facial grammar same as other SLs
EAST ASIAN SIGN LANGUAGE FEATURES
Both CSL and KSL: number signs reflect East Asian counting system
Both: some signs iconic from respective written scripts
Both: mouthing from spoken national language frequent
Both: same five parameters as all sign languages (universal cherology)
PLATE 65b ALT · CSL / KSL · CHEROLOGY
